content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** integers (1‚Č§Ai‚Č§2‚čÖN)(1 \le A_i \le 2 \cdot N)(1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖN), we have to perform KKK moves.
We perform the following steps in one move:


Select an integer XXX such that 1‚Č§X‚Č§2‚čÖN1 \le X \le 2 \cdot N1‚Č§X‚Č§2‚čÖN and X‚Č†AiX \ne A_iXÓÄ†=Ai‚Äč for all iii (X(X(X is not equal to any element of the current array))).
Append XXX to AAA.
The score of this move =(max‚Ā°1‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£A‚ą£Ai)‚ąíX= (\max_{1 \le i \le |A|} A_i) - X=(max1‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£A‚ą£‚ÄčAi‚Äč)‚ąíX.

Note that the score is calculated after appending XXX. Thus, XXX can be the maximum element of the array as well.
For e.g. if A=[3,1,5]A = [3, 1, 5]A=[3,1,5] and we append X=2X = 2X=2 to AAA, then, AAA becomes [3,1,5,2][3, 1, 5, 2][3,1,5,2] and the score of this move is max‚Ā°([3,1,5,2])‚ąí2=5‚ąí2=3\max([3, 1, 5, 2]) - 2 = 5 - 2 = 3max([3,1,5,2])‚ąí2=5‚ąí2=3.

Find the **maximum sum** of scores we can achieve after performing **exactly** KKK moves.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the size of the array AAA and the number of moves respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum sum** of scores we can achieve after performing **exactly** KKK moves.

It is guaranteed that it is always possible to perform KKK moves under the given constraints.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can perform the following move:

Append X=2X = 2X=2. Score of this move =max‚Ā°([1,3,6,2])‚ąí2=6‚ąí2=4= \max([1, 3, 6, 2]) - 2 = 6 - 2 = 4=max([1,3,6,2])‚ąí2=6‚ąí2=4

Hence, the maximum total score is 444.
Test Case 2: We can perform the following moves:

Append X=4X = 4X=4. Score of this move =max‚Ā°([1,2,4])‚ąí4=4‚ąí4=0= \max([1, 2, 4]) - 4 = 4 - 4 = 0=max([1,2,4])‚ąí4=4‚ąí4=0
Append X=3X = 3X=3. Score of this move =max‚Ā°([1,2,4,3])‚ąí3=4‚ąí3=1= \max([1, 2, 4, 3]) - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1=max([1,2,4,3])‚ąí3=4‚ąí3=1

Hence, the maximum total score is 0+1=10+1=10+1=1.
Test Case 3: We can perform the following moves:

Append X=4X = 4X=4. Score of this move =max‚Ā°([1,2,5,4])‚ąí4=5‚ąí4=1= \max([1, 2, 5, 4]) - 4 = 5 - 4 = 1=max([1,2,5,4])‚ąí4=5‚ąí4=1
Append X=3X = 3X=3. Score of this move =max‚Ā°([1,2,5,4,3])‚ąí3=5‚ąí3=2= \max([1, 2, 5, 4, 3]) - 3 = 5 - 3 = 2=max([1,2,5,4,3])‚ąí3=5‚ąí3=2

Hence the maximum total score is 1+2=31+2=31+2=3.
